Diet_Recommendations: Maintain hydration and a balanced diet to support recovery; treat with recommended antimalarials. Emphasize adequate calories and protein during convalescence.
Ensure adequate hydration and easy-to-digest nutritious foods (soups, rice, lean protein) during treatment. Follow full antibiotic course and gradually return to normal diet as tolerated.
Ensure adequate hydration (oral/IV as directed) and maintain light nutritious meals; avoid NSAIDs (aspirin, ibuprofen) due to bleeding risk. Focus on easily digestible foods to maintain strength.
Maintain hydration, consume balanced meals and adequate protein to support immune function. Small frequent meals can help if appetite is low; rest and treat fever per advice.
Stay hydrated, consume vitamin-rich soups and easy-to-digest foods, and rest. Include fruits, vegetables, and warm fluids to ease symptoms.

Maintain hydration and a balanced diet rich in fruits, vegetables, whole grains, and lean proteins. Small frequent meals may help if appetite is reduced; follow clinician advice for supplements if recommended.
Ensure good caloric intake and adequate protein to support recovery. Maintain hydration and consume nutrient-dense, easy-to-eat foods if appetite is reduced.

Maintain a balanced, nutrient-dense diet: plenty of vegetables and fruits, whole grains, lean proteins, healthy fats, and adequate hydration. Limit processed foods, excessive sugar, and excessive alcohol; consult a registered dietitian for disease-specific personalized guidance.

Follow a balanced diet rich in anti-inflammatory foods (fruits, vegetables, omega-3 sources) and avoid highly processed foods. Tailor to tolerance and medical guidance.

Eat a high-calorie, high-protein diet to support weight maintenance and recovery; include fruits, vegetables, and adequate calories during treatment.
